mn41:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā*, wandering on tour among the Kosalans together with a large *saṅgha* of *bhikkhus* [*bhikkhusaṅghena*], arrived at Sālā, a brahmin village of the Kosalans.
mn41:10.1: And how, householders, are there three kinds of conduct contrary to *dhamma* and wrong conduct by mind [*manasā*]?
mn41:10.2: Here, householders, someone is covetous. Whatever belongs to another, another’s property and goods, he covets it: ‘Oh, if only what belongs to another might be mine.’
mn41:10.3: And he has a mind of ill will [*byāpannacitto*], with intentions of malice: ‘May these beings be killed or slaughtered or annihilated or destroyed, or may they not exist!’
mn41:10.4: And he has wrong view, distorted vision:
mn41:10.5: ‘There is no giving, no sacrifice, no offering; there is no fruit or result of good and bad *kamma*s; there is no this world, no other world; there is no mother, no father; there are no beings who are spontaneously reborn; there are no ascetics and brahmins in the world who are practicing rightly and conducting themselves rightly, who, having directly known and realized for themselves this world and the next world, proclaim them.’
mn41:10.6: Thus, householders, there are three kinds of conduct contrary to *dhamma* and wrong conduct by mind [*manasā*].
mn41:10.7: Thus, because of conduct contrary to *dhamma* and wrong conduct, householders, some beings here, with the breakup of the body, after death, are reborn in a plane of loss, a bad destination, a downfall, hell.
mn41:11.1: There are three kinds of conduct in accordance with *dhamma* and proper conduct by body, four kinds of conduct in accordance with *dhamma* and proper conduct by speech, and three kinds of conduct in accordance with *dhamma* and proper conduct by mind [*manasā*].
mn41:12.1: And how, householders, are there three kinds of conduct in accordance with *dhamma* and proper conduct by body?
mn41:12.2: Here, householders, someone, having given up killing living beings, abstains from killing living beings; with rod laid aside and weapon laid aside, conscientious, compassionate, he dwells caring for the welfare of all living beings.
mn41:12.3: Having given up taking what is not given, he abstains from taking what is not given. Whatever belongs to another, another’s property and goods, whether in village or forest, he does not take as a thief what has not been given.
mn41:12.4: Having given up sexual misconduct, he abstains from sexual misconduct. With those protected by mother, protected by father, protected by mother and father, protected by brother, protected by sister, protected by relatives, protected by clan, protected by *dhamma*, who have a husband, whose transgression incurs punishment, even those already garlanded, with such women he does not engage in misconduct.
mn41:12.5: Thus, householders, there are three kinds of conduct in accordance with *dhamma* and proper conduct by body.
mn41:13.1: And how, householders, are there four kinds of conduct in accordance with *dhamma* and proper conduct by speech?
mn41:13.2: Here, householders, someone, having given up false speech, abstains from false speech. Whether he has gone to an assembly, to a gathering, into the midst of relatives, into the midst of a guild, or into the midst of the royal court, when he is brought in and questioned as a witness, ‘Come now, good man, say what you know,’ then, not knowing, he says, ‘I do not know’; or, knowing, he says, ‘I know’; not seeing, he says, ‘I do not see’; or, seeing, he says, ‘I see.’ Thus, for his own sake, or for another’s sake, or for some trivial material gain, he does not knowingly speak falsehood.
mn41:13.3: Having given up divisive speech, he abstains from divisive speech. Having heard something here, he does not tell it there to divide these from those; or having heard something there, he does not tell it here to divide those from these. Thus he is one who reunites the divided, an encourager of the united; he delights in harmony, enjoys harmony, rejoices in harmony, and speaks words that create harmony.
mn41:13.4: Having given up harsh speech, he abstains from harsh speech. He speaks words that are gentle, pleasing to the ear, lovable, going to the heart, courteous, desired by many, agreeable to many.
mn41:13.5: Having given up idle chatter, he abstains from idle chatter. He speaks at the proper time, speaks what is true, speaks what is beneficial, speaks *dhamma*, speaks *vinaya*. He speaks words worth treasuring, timely, well-grounded, measured, and connected with benefit.
mn41:13.6: Thus, householders, there are four kinds of conduct in accordance with *dhamma* and proper conduct by speech.
mn41:14.1: And how, householders, are there three kinds of conduct in accordance with *dhamma* and proper conduct by mind [*manasā*]?
mn41:14.2: Here, householders, someone is not covetous. Whatever belongs to another, another’s property and goods, he does not covet it: ‘Oh, if only what belongs to another might be mine.’
mn41:14.3: And he has a mind free of ill will [*abyāpannacitto*], with intentions free of malice: ‘May these beings be without enmity, without affliction, free from trouble, and may they look after themselves happily.’
mn41:14.4: And he has right view, undistorted vision:
mn41:14.5: ‘There is giving, there is sacrifice, there is offering; there is fruit and result of good and bad *kamma*s; there is this world, there is the other world; there is mother, there is father; there are beings who are spontaneously reborn; there are ascetics and brahmins in the world who are practicing rightly and conducting themselves rightly, who, having directly known and realized for themselves this world and the next world, proclaim them.’
mn41:14.6: Thus, householders, there are three kinds of conduct in accordance with *dhamma* and proper conduct by mind [*manasā*].
mn41:14.7: Thus, because of conduct in accordance with *dhamma* and proper conduct, householders, some beings here, with the breakup of the body, after death, are reborn in a good destination, a heavenly world.
mn41:15.1: If, householders, one who lives in accordance with *dhamma* and conducts himself properly were to wish:
mn41:15.2: ‘Oh, may I, with the breakup of the body, after death, be reborn in the company of wealthy nobles,’
mn41:15.3: it is possible that, with the breakup of the body, after death, he would be reborn in the company of wealthy nobles.
mn41:15.5: Because he lives in accordance with *dhamma* and conducts himself properly.
mn41:16-17.1: If, householders, one who lives in accordance with *dhamma* and conducts himself properly were to wish:
mn41:16-17.2: ‘Oh, may I, with the breakup of the body, after death, be reborn in the company of wealthy brahmins …
mn41:16-17.4: it is possible that, with the breakup of the body, after death, he would be reborn in the company of wealthy householders.
mn41:16-17.6: Because he lives in accordance with *dhamma* and conducts himself properly.
mn41:18-42.1: If, householders, one who lives in accordance with *dhamma* and conducts himself properly were to wish:
mn41:18-42.10: the devas who delight in creating …
mn41:18-42.11: the devas who wield power over the creations of others …
mn41:18-42.13: it is possible that, with the breakup of the body, after death, he would be reborn in the company of the Brahmā-host devas.
mn41:18-42.15: Because he lives in accordance with *dhamma* and conducts himself properly.
mn41:18-42.16: If, householders, one who lives in accordance with *dhamma* and conducts himself properly were to wish:
mn41:18-42.17: ‘Oh, may I, with the breakup of the body, after death, be reborn in the company of the Radiant devas,’
mn41:18-42.18: it is possible that, with the breakup of the body, after death, he would be reborn in the company of the Radiant devas.
mn41:18-42.2: ‘Oh, may I, with the breakup of the body, after death, be reborn in the company of the devas of the Four Great Kings,’
mn41:18-42.20: Because he lives in accordance with *dhamma* and conducts himself properly.
mn41:18-42.21: If, householders, one who lives in accordance with *dhamma* and conducts himself properly were to wish:
mn41:18-42.22: ‘Oh, may I, with the breakup of the body, after death, be reborn in the company of the devas of Limited Radiance …
mn41:18-42.3: it is possible that, with the breakup of the body, after death, he would be reborn in the company of the devas of the Four Great Kings.
mn41:18-42.34: the devas who have gone to the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanūpagānaṁ*] …
mn41:18-42.35: the devas who have gone to the dimension of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanūpagānaṁ*] …
mn41:18-42.36: the devas who have gone to the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanūpagānaṁ*] …
mn41:18-42.37: the devas who have gone to the dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanūpagānaṁ*],’
mn41:18-42.38: it is possible that, with the breakup of the body, after death, he would be reborn in the company of the devas who have gone to the dimension of neither *saññā* nor non-*saññā* [*nevasaññānāsaññāyatanūpagānaṁ*].
mn41:18-42.40: Because he lives in accordance with *dhamma* and conducts himself properly.
mn41:18-42.5: Because he lives in accordance with *dhamma* and conducts himself properly.
mn41:18-42.6: If, householders, one who lives in accordance with *dhamma* and conducts himself properly were to wish:
mn41:18-42.7: ‘Oh, may I, with the breakup of the body, after death, be reborn in the company of the Tāvatiṁsa devas …
mn41:2.1: The brahmin householders of Sālā heard:
mn41:2.2: “Sirs, the ascetic Gotama, the Sakyan son who went forth from a Sakyan clan, wandering on tour among the Kosalans together with a large *saṅgha* of *bhikkhus* [*bhikkhusaṅghena*], has arrived at Sālā.
mn41:2.3: And this good report about Master Gotama has been spread around:
mn41:2.4: ‘That *Bhagavā* is an *arahant*, a perfectly awakened one, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, fortunate, knower of the world, unsurpassed trainer of persons to be tamed, teacher of devas and humans, awakened, the *Bhagavā*.’
mn41:2.5: He proclaims this world with its devas, Māra, and Brahmā, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its devas and humans, having directly known and realized it for himself.
mn41:2.6: He teaches the *dhamma* that is good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, with meaning and wording;
mn41:2.7: he makes known the holy life that is entirely full and pure.
mn41:2.8: It is good indeed to see such *arahants*.”
mn41:3.1: Then the brahmin householders of Sālā went to the *Bhagavā*. Having gone, some paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side; some exchanged greetings with the *Bhagavā*, and after conducting a courteous and memorable conversation, sat down to one side; some raised their joined palms toward the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side; some announced their name and clan in the presence of the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side; some sat down to one side in silence. Sitting to one side, the brahmin householders of Sālā said this to the *Bhagavā*:
mn41:4.1: “What, Master Gotama, is the cause, what is the condition, whereby some beings here, with the breakup of the body, after death, are reborn in a plane of loss, a bad destination, a downfall, hell?
mn41:4.2: And what, Master Gotama, is the cause, what is the condition, whereby some beings here, with the breakup of the body, after death, are reborn in a good destination, a heavenly world?”
mn41:43.1: If, householders, one who lives in accordance with *dhamma* and conducts himself properly were to wish:
mn41:43.2: ‘Oh, may I, with the destruction of the *āsavas*, in this very life dwell having realized for myself with direct knowledge, having entered upon the liberation of mind and liberation by discernment without *āsavas* [*anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ*],’
mn41:43.3: it is possible that, with the destruction of the *āsavas*, in this very life he would dwell having realized for himself with direct knowledge, having entered upon the liberation of mind and liberation by discernment without *āsavas* [*anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ*].
mn41:43.5: Because he lives in accordance with *dhamma* and conducts himself properly.”
mn41:44.1: When this was said, the brahmin householders of Sālā said this to the *Bhagavā*:
mn41:44.3: Just as if one might set upright what had been overturned, reveal what had been hidden, point out the way to one who was lost, or hold up an oil lamp in the darkness so that those with eyes could see forms, so too Master Gotama has made the *dhamma* clear in many ways.
mn41:44.4: We go for refuge to Master Gotama, to the *dhamma*, and to the *saṅgha* of *bhikkhus* [*bhikkhusaṅghaṁ*].
mn41:44.5: Let Master Gotama remember us as lay followers who from today have gone for refuge for life.”
mn41:44.6: The first, The People of Sālā, is finished.
mn41:5.1: “Because of conduct contrary to *dhamma* and wrong conduct, householders, some beings here, with the breakup of the body, after death, are reborn in a plane of loss, a bad destination, a downfall, hell.
mn41:5.2: Because of conduct in accordance with *dhamma* and proper conduct, householders, some beings here, with the breakup of the body, after death, are reborn in a good destination, a heavenly world.”
mn41:6.1: “We do not understand in detail the meaning of this brief statement by Master Gotama, whose meaning has not been analyzed in detail.
mn41:6.2: It would be good if Master Gotama would teach us the *dhamma* in such a way that we might understand in detail the meaning of this brief statement by Master Gotama, whose meaning has not been analyzed in detail.”
mn41:6.3: “Then, householders, listen and attend closely. I will speak.”
mn41:6.4: “Yes, sir,” the brahmin householders of Sālā replied to the *Bhagavā*.
mn41:7.1: “There are three kinds of conduct contrary to *dhamma* and wrong conduct by body, four kinds of conduct contrary to *dhamma* and wrong conduct by speech, and three kinds of conduct contrary to *dhamma* and wrong conduct by mind [*manasā*].
mn41:8.1: And how, householders, are there three kinds of conduct contrary to *dhamma* and wrong conduct by body?
mn41:8.2: Here, householders, someone kills living beings; he is cruel, bloody-handed, devoted to beating and slaying, merciless toward living beings.
mn41:8.3: And he takes what is not given. Whatever belongs to another, another’s property and goods, whether in village or forest, he takes as a thief what has not been given.
mn41:8.4: And he engages in sexual misconduct. With those protected by mother, protected by father, protected by mother and father, protected by brother, protected by sister, protected by relatives, protected by clan, protected by *dhamma*, who have a husband, whose transgression incurs punishment, even those already garlanded, with such women he engages in misconduct.
mn41:8.5: Thus, householders, there are three kinds of conduct contrary to *dhamma* and wrong conduct by body.
mn41:9.1: And how, householders, are there four kinds of conduct contrary to *dhamma* and wrong conduct by speech?
mn41:9.2: Here, householders, someone is a liar. Whether he has gone to an assembly, to a gathering, into the midst of relatives, into the midst of a guild, or into the midst of the royal court, when he is brought in and questioned as a witness, ‘Come now, good man, say what you know,’ then, not knowing, he says, ‘I know’; or, knowing, he says, ‘I do not know’; not seeing, he says, ‘I see’; or, seeing, he says, ‘I do not see.’ Thus, for his own sake, or for another’s sake, or for some trivial material gain, he knowingly speaks falsehood.
mn41:9.3: And he speaks divisively. Having heard something here, he tells it there to divide these from those; or having heard something there, he tells it here to divide those from these. Thus he is one who splits up the united, a promoter of the divided; he delights in factions, enjoys factions, rejoices in factions, and speaks words that create factions.
mn41:9.4: And he speaks harshly. He speaks words that are rough, harsh, hurtful to others, abusive, bordering on anger, not leading to *samādhi* [*asamādhisaṁvattanikā*].
mn41:9.5: And he indulges in idle chatter. He speaks at the wrong time, speaks what is untrue, speaks what is unbeneficial, speaks contrary to *dhamma*, speaks contrary to *vinaya*. He speaks words not worth treasuring, untimely, baseless, unbounded, and disconnected from benefit.
mn41:9.6: Thus, householders, there are four kinds of conduct contrary to *dhamma* and wrong conduct by speech.
